papers.title: SelfPlan: Learning Mid-to-Mid Motion Planning through Closed-Map Self-Play
papers.abstract: SelfPlan is a closed-map scalable self-play framework for learning mid-to-mid motion planners. The policy outputs short-horizon trajectories tracked by a low-level controller, using structured ego-frame observations and rewards for path quality, efficiency, safety, and goal reaching. Evaluation spans CARLA closed maps, Waymo validation maps, and Waymo Open Sim Agents realism metrics.

papers.title: Motion Planning in Urban Environments via Self-Play Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: This work studies self-play reinforcement learning for mid-to-mid motion planning in urban autonomous driving. The planner maps structured observations to continuous trajectories parameterized by cubic Hermite splines, preserving a modular perception-planning-control pipeline. Training is performed in the GPU-accelerated PufferDrive simulator, where all agents share and continuously update one policy to generate progressively harder interactions without manual scenario design.
papers.keyResult: 92.2% task completion on WAMO validation set with 340k steps/s
